description: Cal.com v2 API — scheduling infrastructure. Authenticate with a Bearer token (API key from Settings > Developer > API Keys). Supports per-endpoint versioning via cal-api-version header.

params.name: apiKeyDaysValid
params.description: For how many days is managed organization api key valid. Defaults to 30 days.

params.name: apiKeyNeverExpires
params.description: If true, organization api key never expires.

tools.name: auth_oauth2-token
tools.description: Exchange authorization code or refresh token for tokens

params.name: status
params.description: Filter bookings by status. If you want to filter by multiple statuses, separate them with a comma.

params.name: attendeeEmail
params.description: Filter bookings by the attendee's email address.

params.name: attendeeName
params.description: Filter bookings by the attendee's name.

params.name: bookingUid
params.description: Filter bookings by the booking Uid.

params.name: eventTypeIds
params.description: Filter bookings by event type ids belonging to the user. Event type ids must be separated by a comma.

params.name: eventTypeId
params.description: Filter bookings by event type id belonging to the user.

params.name: teamsIds
params.description: Filter bookings by team ids that user is part of. Team ids must be separated by a comma.

params.name: teamId
params.description: Filter bookings by team id that user is part of

params.name: afterStart
params.description: Filter bookings with start after this date string.

params.name: beforeEnd
params.description: Filter bookings with end before this date string.

params.name: afterCreatedAt
params.description: Filter bookings that have been created after this date string.

params.name: beforeCreatedAt
params.description: Filter bookings that have been created before this date string.

params.name: afterUpdatedAt
params.description: Filter bookings that have been updated after this date string.

params.name: beforeUpdatedAt
params.description: Filter bookings that have been updated before this date string.

params.name: sortStart
params.description: Sort results by their start time in ascending or descending order.

params.name: sortEnd
params.description: Sort results by their end time in ascending or descending order.

params.name: sortCreated
params.description: Sort results by their creation time (when booking was made) in ascending or descending order.

params.name: sortUpdatedAt
params.description: Sort results by their updated time (for example when booking status changes) in ascending or descending order.

params.name: take
params.description: The number of items to return

params.name: skip
params.description: The number of items to skip

params.name: externalId
params.description: Unique identifier used to represent the specific calendar, as returned by the /calendars endpoint

params.name: integration
params.description: The calendar service you want to integrate, as returned by the /calendars endpoint

params.name: afterEventBuffer
params.description: Extra time automatically blocked on your calendar after a meeting ends. This gives you time to wrap up, add notes, or decompress before your next commitment.

params.name: allowReschedulingCancelledBookings
params.description: When enabled, users will be able to create a new booking when trying to reschedule a cancelled booking.

params.name: allowReschedulingPastBookings
params.description: Enabling this option allows for past events to be rescheduled.

params.name: beforeEventBuffer
params.description: Extra time automatically blocked on your calendar before a meeting starts. This gives you time to prepare, review notes, or transition from your previous activity.

params.name: bookerActiveBookingsLimit
params.description: Limit the number of active bookings a booker can make for this event type.

params.name: bookingLimitsCount
params.description: Limit how many times this event can be booked

params.name: bookingLimitsDuration
params.description: Limit total amount of time that this event can be booked

params.name: bookingRequiresAuthentication
params.description: Boolean to require authentication for booking this event type via api. If true, only authenticated users who are the event-type owner or org/team admin/owner can book this event type.

params.name: bookingWindow
params.description: Limit how far in the future this event can be booked

params.name: confirmationPolicy
params.description: Specify how the booking needs to be manually confirmed before it is pushed to the integrations and a confirmation mail is sent.

params.name: customName
params.description:
Customizable event name with valid variables:
      {Event type title}, {Organiser}, {Scheduler}, {Location}, {Organiser first name},
      {Scheduler first name}, {Scheduler last name}, {Event duration}, {LOCATION},
      {HOST/ATTENDEE}, {HOST}, {ATTENDEE}, {USER}

params.name: disableGuests
params.description: If true, person booking this event can't add guests via their emails.

params.name: hideOrganizerEmail
params.description: Boolean to Hide organizer's email address from the booking screen, email notifications, and calendar events

params.name: interfaceLanguage
params.description: Set preferred language for the booking interface. Use empty string for visitor's browser language (default).

params.name: lengthInMinutesOptions
params.description: If you want that user can choose between different lengths of the event you can specify them here. Must include the provided `lengthInMinutes`.

params.name: lockTimeZoneToggleOnBookingPage
params.description: Lock time zone toggle on booking page

params.name: minimumBookingNotice
params.description: Minimum number of minutes before the event that a booking can be made.

params.name: offsetStart
params.description: Offset timeslots shown to bookers by a specified number of minutes

params.name: onlyShowFirstAvailableSlot
params.description: This will limit your availability for this event type to one slot per day, scheduled at the earliest available time.

params.name: scheduleId
params.description: If you want that this event has different schedule than user's default one you can specify it here.

params.name: seats
params.description: Create an event type with multiple seats.

params.name: showOptimizedSlots
params.description: Arrange time slots to optimize availability.

params.name: slotInterval
params.description:
Number representing length of each slot when event is booked. By default it equal length of the event type.
      If event length is 60 minutes then we would have slots 9AM, 10AM, 11AM etc. but if it was changed to 30 minutes then
      we would have slots 9AM, 9:30AM, 10AM, 10:30AM etc. as the available times to book the 60 minute event.

params.name: successRedirectUrl
params.description: A valid URL where the booker will redirect to, once the booking is completed successfully

params.name: username
params.description: The username of the user to get event types for. If only username provided will get all event types.

params.name: eventSlug
params.description: Slug of event type to return. Notably, if eventSlug is provided then username must be provided too, because multiple users can have event with same slug.

params.name: usernames
params.description: Get dynamic event type for multiple usernames separated by comma. e.g `usernames=alice,bob`

params.name: orgSlug
params.description: slug of the user's organization if he is in one, orgId is not required if using this parameter

params.name: orgId
params.description: ID of the organization of the user you want the get the event-types of, orgSlug is not needed when using this parameter

params.name: sortCreatedAt
params.description: Sort event types by creation date. When not provided, no explicit ordering is applied.

params.description: Extra time automatically blocked on your calendar after a meeting ends. This gives you time to wrap up, add notes, or decompress before your next commitment.

params.description: When enabled, users will be able to create a new booking when trying to reschedule a cancelled booking.

params.description: Enabling this option allows for past events to be rescheduled.

params.description: Extra time automatically blocked on your calendar before a meeting starts. This gives you time to prepare, review notes, or transition from your previous activity.

params.description: URL of the user's avatar image

params.name: areCalendarEventsEnabled
params.description: If true and if managed user has calendar connected, calendar events will be created. Disable it if you manually create calendar events. Default to true.

params.name: areDefaultEventTypesEnabled
params.description: If true, when creating a managed user the managed user will have 4 default event types: 30 and 60 minutes without Cal video, 30 and 60 minutes with Cal video. Set this as false if you want to create a managed user and then manually create event types for the user.

params.name: permissions
params.description: Array of permission keys like ["BOOKING_READ", "BOOKING_WRITE"]. Use ["*"] to grant all permissions.

params.name: areCalendarEventsEnabled
params.description: If true and if managed user has calendar connected, calendar events will be created. Disable it if you manually create calendar events. Default to true.

params.name: areDefaultEventTypesEnabled
params.description: If true, when creating a managed user the managed user will have 4 default event types: 30 and 60 minutes without Cal video, 30 and 60 minutes with Cal video. Set this as false if you want to create a managed user and then manually create event types for the user.

params.name: avatarUrl
params.description: URL of the user's avatar image

params.name: name
params.description: Managed user's name is used in emails

params.name: timeZone
params.description:
Timezone is used to create user's default schedule from Monday to Friday from 9AM to 5PM. If it is not passed then user does not have
      a default schedule and it must be created manually via the /schedules endpoint. Until the schedule is created, the user can't access availability atom to set his / her availability nor booked.
      It will default to Europe/London if not passed.

tools.name: organizations_routing-forms_organizations-responses-create-response
tools.description: Create routing form response and get available slots

params.name: start
params.description:
Time starting from which available slots should be checked.
    
      Must be in UTC timezone as ISO 8601 datestring.
      
      You can pass date without hours which defaults to start of day or specify hours:
      2024-08-13 (will have hours 00:00:00 aka at very beginning of the date) or you can specify hours manually like 2024-08-13T09:00:00Z

params.name: end
params.description:
Time until which available slots should be checked.
      
      Must be in UTC timezone as ISO 8601 datestring.
      
      You can pass date without hours which defaults to end of day or specify hours:
      2024-08-20 (will have hours 23:59:59 aka at the very end of the date) or you can specify hours manually like 2024-08-20T18:00:00Z

params.name: timeZone
params.description: Time zone in which the available slots should be returned. Defaults to UTC.

params.name: duration
params.description: If event type has multiple possible durations then you can specify the desired duration here. Also, if you are fetching slots for a dynamic event then you can specify the duration her which defaults to 30, meaning that returned slots will be each 30 minutes long.

params.name: format
params.description: Format of slot times in response. Use 'range' to get start and end times.

params.name: bookingUidToReschedule
params.description: The unique identifier of the booking being rescheduled. When provided will ensure that the original booking time appears within the returned available slots when rescheduling.

params.name: queueResponse
params.description: Whether to queue the form response.

params.name: afterCreatedAt
params.description: Filter by responses created after this date

params.name: beforeCreatedAt
params.description: Filter by responses created before this date

params.name: afterUpdatedAt
params.description: Filter by responses created after this date

params.name: beforeUpdatedAt
params.description: Filter by responses updated before this date

params.name: routedToBookingUid
params.description: Filter by responses routed to a specific booking

params.name: take
params.description: Maximum number of items to return

params.name: autoAcceptCreator
params.description: If you are a platform customer, don't pass 'false', because then team creator won't be able to create team event types.

params.name: bannerUrl
params.description: URL of the teams banner image which is shown on booker

params.name: logoUrl
params.description: URL of the teams logo image

params.name: slug
params.description: Team slug in kebab-case - if not provided will be generated automatically based on name.

params.name: timeZone
params.description: Timezone is used to create teams's default schedule from Monday to Friday from 9AM to 5PM. It will default to Europe/London if not passed.

params.name: afterEventBuffer
params.description: Extra time automatically blocked on your calendar after a meeting ends. This gives you time to wrap up, add notes, or decompress before your next commitment.

params.name: allowReschedulingCancelledBookings
params.description: When enabled, users will be able to create a new booking when trying to reschedule a cancelled booking.

params.name: allowReschedulingPastBookings
params.description: Enabling this option allows for past events to be rescheduled.

params.name: assignAllTeamMembers
params.description: If true, all current and future team members will be assigned to this event type. Provide either assignAllTeamMembers or hosts but not both

params.name: beforeEventBuffer
params.description: Extra time automatically blocked on your calendar before a meeting starts. This gives you time to prepare, review notes, or transition from your previous activity.

params.name: bookerActiveBookingsLimit
params.description: Limit the number of active bookings a booker can make for this event type.

params.name: bookingLimitsCount
params.description: Limit how many times this event can be booked

params.name: bookingLimitsDuration
params.description: Limit total amount of time that this event can be booked

params.name: bookingRequiresAuthentication
params.description: Boolean to require authentication for booking this event type via api. If true, only authenticated users who are the event-type owner or org/team admin/owner can book this event type.

params.name: bookingWindow
params.description: Limit how far in the future this event can be booked

params.name: confirmationPolicy
params.description: Specify how the booking needs to be manually confirmed before it is pushed to the integrations and a confirmation mail is sent.

params.name: customName
params.description:
Customizable event name with valid variables:
      {Event type title}, {Organiser}, {Scheduler}, {Location}, {Organiser first name},
      {Scheduler first name}, {Scheduler last name}, {Event duration}, {LOCATION},
      {HOST/ATTENDEE}, {HOST}, {ATTENDEE}, {USER}

params.name: disableGuests
params.description: If true, person booking this event can't add guests via their emails.

params.name: hideOrganizerEmail
params.description: Boolean to Hide organizer's email address from the booking screen, email notifications, and calendar events

params.name: interfaceLanguage
params.description: Set preferred language for the booking interface. Use empty string for visitor's browser language (default).

params.name: lengthInMinutesOptions
params.description: If you want that user can choose between different lengths of the event you can specify them here. Must include the provided `lengthInMinutes`.

params.name: lockTimeZoneToggleOnBookingPage
params.description: Lock time zone toggle on booking page

params.name: minimumBookingNotice
params.description: Minimum number of minutes before the event that a booking can be made.

params.name: offsetStart
params.description: Offset timeslots shown to bookers by a specified number of minutes

params.name: onlyShowFirstAvailableSlot
params.description: This will limit your availability for this event type to one slot per day, scheduled at the earliest available time.

params.name: rescheduleWithSameRoundRobinHost
params.description: Rescheduled events will be assigned to the same host as initially scheduled.

params.name: scheduleId
params.description: If you want that this event has different schedule than user's default one you can specify it here.

params.name: schedulingType
params.description: The scheduling type for the team event - collective, roundRobin or managed.

params.name: seats
params.description: Create an event type with multiple seats.

params.name: showOptimizedSlots
params.description: Arrange time slots to optimize availability.

params.name: slotInterval
params.description:
Number representing length of each slot when event is booked. By default it equal length of the event type.
      If event length is 60 minutes then we would have slots 9AM, 10AM, 11AM etc. but if it was changed to 30 minutes then
      we would have slots 9AM, 9:30AM, 10AM, 10:30AM etc. as the available times to book the 60 minute event.

params.name: successRedirectUrl
params.description: A valid URL where the booker will redirect to, once the booking is completed successfully

params.name: take
params.description: Maximum number of items to return

params.name: sortCreatedAt
params.description: Sort event types by creation date. When not provided, no explicit ordering is applied.

params.name: eventSlug
params.description: Slug of team event type to return.

params.name: hostsLimit
params.description: Specifies the maximum number of hosts to include in the response. This limit helps optimize performance. If not provided, all Hosts will be fetched.

params.name: sortCreatedAt
params.description: Sort event types by creation date. When not provided, no explicit ordering is applied.

params.name: schedulingType
params.description:
The scheduling type for the team event - collective or roundRobin. ❗If you change scheduling type you must also provide `hosts` or `assignAllTeamMembers` in the request body, otherwise the event type will have no hosts - this is required because
      in case of collective event type all hosts are mandatory but in case of round robin some or non can be mandatory so we can't predict how you want the hosts to be setup which is why you must provide that information.  If you want to convert round robin or collective into managed or managed into round robin or collective then you will have to create a new team event type and delete old one.

params.name: slotInterval
params.description:
Number representing length of each slot when event is booked. By default it equal length of the event type.
      If event length is 60 minutes then we would have slots 9AM, 10AM, 11AM etc. but if it was changed to 30 minutes then
      we would have slots 9AM, 9:30AM, 10AM, 10:30AM etc. as the available times to book the 60 minute event.

tools.name: organizations_teams_organizations-routing-forms-responses-create-routing-form-response
tools.description: Create routing form response and get available slots

params.name: start
params.description:
Time starting from which available slots should be checked.
    
      Must be in UTC timezone as ISO 8601 datestring.
      
      You can pass date without hours which defaults to start of day or specify hours:
      2024-08-13 (will have hours 00:00:00 aka at very beginning of the date) or you can specify hours manually like 2024-08-13T09:00:00Z

params.name: end
params.description:
Time until which available slots should be checked.
      
      Must be in UTC timezone as ISO 8601 datestring.
      
      You can pass date without hours which defaults to end of day or specify hours:
      2024-08-20 (will have hours 23:59:59 aka at the very end of the date) or you can specify hours manually like 2024-08-20T18:00:00Z

params.name: timeZone
params.description: Time zone in which the available slots should be returned. Defaults to UTC.

params.name: duration
params.description: If event type has multiple possible durations then you can specify the desired duration here. Also, if you are fetching slots for a dynamic event then you can specify the duration her which defaults to 30, meaning that returned slots will be each 30 minutes long.

params.name: format
params.description: Format of slot times in response. Use 'range' to get start and end times.

params.name: bookingUidToReschedule
params.description: The unique identifier of the booking being rescheduled. When provided will ensure that the original booking time appears within the returned available slots when rescheduling.

params.name: queueResponse
params.description: Whether to queue the form response.

tools.name: routing-forms_calculate-slots_routing-forms-based-on-routing-form-response
tools.description: Calculate slots based on routing form response

params.name: start
params.description:
Time starting from which available slots should be checked.
    
      Must be in UTC timezone as ISO 8601 datestring.
      
      You can pass date without hours which defaults to start of day or specify hours:
      2024-08-13 (will have hours 00:00:00 aka at very beginning of the date) or you can specify hours manually like 2024-08-13T09:00:00Z

params.name: end
params.description:
Time until which available slots should be checked.
      
      Must be in UTC timezone as ISO 8601 datestring.
      
      You can pass date without hours which defaults to end of day or specify hours:
      2024-08-20 (will have hours 23:59:59 aka at the very end of the date) or you can specify hours manually like 2024-08-20T18:00:00Z

params.name: timeZone
params.description: Time zone in which the available slots should be returned. Defaults to UTC.

params.name: duration
params.description: If event type has multiple possible durations then you can specify the desired duration here. Also, if you are fetching slots for a dynamic event then you can specify the duration her which defaults to 30, meaning that returned slots will be each 30 minutes long.

params.name: format
params.description: Format of slot times in response. Use 'range' to get start and end times.

params.name: bookingUidToReschedule
params.description: The unique identifier of the booking being rescheduled. When provided will ensure that the original booking time appears within the returned available slots when rescheduling.

params.name: isDefault
params.description:
Each user should have 1 default schedule. If you specified `timeZone` when creating managed user, then the default schedule will be created with that timezone.
    Default schedule means that if an event type is not tied to a specific schedule then the default schedule is used.

params.name: timeZone
params.description: Timezone is used to calculate available times when an event using the schedule is booked.

tools.name: slots_get-available
tools.description: Get available time slots for an event type

params.name: bookingUidToReschedule
params.description: The unique identifier of the booking being rescheduled. When provided will ensure that the original booking time appears within the returned available slots when rescheduling.

params.name: start
params.description:
Time starting from which available slots should be checked.

      Must be in UTC timezone as ISO 8601 datestring.

      You can pass date without hours which defaults to start of day or specify hours:
      2024-08-13 (will have hours 00:00:00 aka at very beginning of the date) or you can specify hours manually like 2024-08-13T09:00:00Z.

params.name: end
params.description:
Time until which available slots should be checked.

    Must be in UTC timezone as ISO 8601 datestring.

    You can pass date without hours which defaults to end of day or specify hours:
    2024-08-20 (will have hours 23:59:59 aka at the very end of the date) or you can specify hours manually like 2024-08-20T18:00:00Z.

params.name: organizationSlug
params.description: The slug of the organization to which user with username belongs or team with teamSlug belongs.

params.name: teamSlug
params.description: The slug of the team who owns event type with eventTypeSlug - used when slots are checked for team event type.

params.name: username
params.description: The username of the user who owns event type with eventTypeSlug - used when slots are checked for individual user event type.

params.name: eventTypeSlug
params.description: The slug of the event type for which available slots should be checked. If slug is provided then username or teamSlug must be provided too and if relevant organizationSlug too.

params.name: eventTypeId
params.description: The ID of the event type for which available slots should be checked.

params.name: usernames
params.description:
The usernames for which available slots should be checked separated by a comma.

    Checking slots by usernames is used mainly for dynamic events where there is no specific event but we just want to know when 2 or more people are available.

    Must contain at least 2 usernames.

params.name: format
params.description: Format of slot times in response. Use 'range' to get start and end times. Use 'time' or omit this query parameter to get only start time.

params.name: duration
params.description: If event type has multiple possible durations then you can specify the desired duration here. Also, if you are fetching slots for a dynamic event then you can specify the duration her which defaults to 30, meaning that returned slots will be each 30 minutes long.

params.name: timeZone
params.description: Time zone in which the available slots should be returned. Defaults to UTC.

params.name: eventTypeId
params.description: The ID of the event type for which slot should be reserved.

params.name: reservationDuration
params.description:
ONLY for authenticated requests with api key, access token or OAuth credentials (ID + secret).
      
      For how many minutes the slot should be reserved - for this long time noone else can book this event type at `start` time. If not provided, defaults to 5 minutes.

params.name: slotDuration
params.description: By default slot duration is equal to event type length, but if you want to reserve a slot for an event type that has a variable length you can specify it here as a number in minutes. If you don't have this set explicitly that event type can have one of many lengths you can omit this.

params.name: slotStart
params.description: ISO 8601 datestring in UTC timezone representing available slot.

params.name: eventTypeId
params.description: The ID of the event type for which slot should be reserved.

params.name: reservationDuration
params.description:
ONLY for authenticated requests with api key, access token or OAuth credentials (ID + secret).
      
      For how many minutes the slot should be reserved - for this long time noone else can book this event type at `start` time. If not provided, defaults to 5 minutes.

params.name: slotDuration
params.description: By default slot duration is equal to event type length, but if you want to reserve a slot for an event type that has a variable length you can specify it here as a number in minutes. If you don't have this set explicitly that event type can have one of many lengths you can omit this.

params.name: slotStart
params.description: ISO 8601 datestring in UTC timezone representing available slot.

params.name: autoAcceptCreator
params.description: If you are a platform customer, don't pass 'false', because then team creator won't be able to create team event types.

params.name: bannerUrl
params.description: URL of the teams banner image which is shown on booker

params.name: logoUrl
params.description: URL of the teams logo image

params.name: slug
params.description: Team slug in kebab-case - if not provided will be generated automatically based on name.

params.name: timeZone
params.description: Timezone is used to create teams's default schedule from Monday to Friday from 9AM to 5PM. It will default to Europe/London if not passed.

params.name: bannerUrl
params.description: URL of the teams banner image which is shown on booker

params.name: logoUrl
params.description: URL of the teams logo image

params.name: timeZone
params.description: Timezone is used to create teams's default schedule from Monday to Friday from 9AM to 5PM. It will default to Europe/London if not passed.

params.name: status
params.description: Filter bookings by status. If you want to filter by multiple statuses, separate them with a comma.

params.name: attendeeEmail
params.description: Filter bookings by the attendee's email address.

params.name: attendeeName
params.description: Filter bookings by the attendee's name.

params.name: bookingUid
params.description: Filter bookings by the booking Uid.

params.name: eventTypeIds
params.description: Filter bookings by event type ids belonging to the team. Event type ids must be separated by a comma.

params.name: eventTypeId
params.description: Filter bookings by event type id belonging to the team.

params.name: afterStart
params.description: Filter bookings with start after this date string.

params.name: beforeEnd
params.description: Filter bookings with end before this date string.

params.name: sortStart
params.description: Sort results by their start time in ascending or descending order.

params.name: sortEnd
params.description: Sort results by their end time in ascending or descending order.

params.name: sortCreated
params.description: Sort results by their creation time (when booking was made) in ascending or descending order.

params.name: take
params.description: The number of items to return

params.name: skip
params.description: The number of items to skip

params.name: afterEventBuffer
params.description: Extra time automatically blocked on your calendar after a meeting ends. This gives you time to wrap up, add notes, or decompress before your next commitment.

params.name: allowReschedulingCancelledBookings
params.description: When enabled, users will be able to create a new booking when trying to reschedule a cancelled booking.

params.name: allowReschedulingPastBookings
params.description: Enabling this option allows for past events to be rescheduled.

params.name: assignAllTeamMembers
params.description: If true, all current and future team members will be assigned to this event type. Provide either assignAllTeamMembers or hosts but not both

params.name: beforeEventBuffer
params.description: Extra time automatically blocked on your calendar before a meeting starts. This gives you time to prepare, review notes, or transition from your previous activity.

params.name: bookerActiveBookingsLimit
params.description: Limit the number of active bookings a booker can make for this event type.

params.name: bookingLimitsCount
params.description: Limit how many times this event can be booked

params.name: bookingLimitsDuration
params.description: Limit total amount of time that this event can be booked

params.name: bookingRequiresAuthentication
params.description: Boolean to require authentication for booking this event type via api. If true, only authenticated users who are the event-type owner or org/team admin/owner can book this event type.

params.name: bookingWindow
params.description: Limit how far in the future this event can be booked

params.name: confirmationPolicy
params.description: Specify how the booking needs to be manually confirmed before it is pushed to the integrations and a confirmation mail is sent.

params.name: customName
params.description:
Customizable event name with valid variables:
      {Event type title}, {Organiser}, {Scheduler}, {Location}, {Organiser first name},
      {Scheduler first name}, {Scheduler last name}, {Event duration}, {LOCATION},
      {HOST/ATTENDEE}, {HOST}, {ATTENDEE}, {USER}

params.name: disableGuests
params.description: If true, person booking this event can't add guests via their emails.

params.name: hideOrganizerEmail
params.description: Boolean to Hide organizer's email address from the booking screen, email notifications, and calendar events

params.name: interfaceLanguage
params.description: Set preferred language for the booking interface. Use empty string for visitor's browser language (default).

params.name: lengthInMinutesOptions
params.description: If you want that user can choose between different lengths of the event you can specify them here. Must include the provided `lengthInMinutes`.

params.name: lockTimeZoneToggleOnBookingPage
params.description: Lock time zone toggle on booking page

params.name: minimumBookingNotice
params.description: Minimum number of minutes before the event that a booking can be made.

params.name: offsetStart
params.description: Offset timeslots shown to bookers by a specified number of minutes

params.name: onlyShowFirstAvailableSlot
params.description: This will limit your availability for this event type to one slot per day, scheduled at the earliest available time.

params.name: rescheduleWithSameRoundRobinHost
params.description: Rescheduled events will be assigned to the same host as initially scheduled.

params.name: scheduleId
params.description: If you want that this event has different schedule than user's default one you can specify it here.

params.name: schedulingType
params.description: The scheduling type for the team event - collective, roundRobin or managed.

params.name: seats
params.description: Create an event type with multiple seats.

params.name: showOptimizedSlots
params.description: Arrange time slots to optimize availability.

params.name: slotInterval
params.description:
Number representing length of each slot when event is booked. By default it equal length of the event type.
      If event length is 60 minutes then we would have slots 9AM, 10AM, 11AM etc. but if it was changed to 30 minutes then
      we would have slots 9AM, 9:30AM, 10AM, 10:30AM etc. as the available times to book the 60 minute event.

params.name: successRedirectUrl
params.description: A valid URL where the booker will redirect to, once the booking is completed successfully

params.name: eventSlug
params.description: Slug of team event type to return.

params.name: hostsLimit
params.description: Specifies the maximum number of hosts to include in the response. This limit helps optimize performance. If not provided, all Hosts will be fetched.

params.name: sortCreatedAt
params.description: Sort event types by creation date. When not provided, no explicit ordering is applied.

params.name: afterEventBuffer
params.description: Extra time automatically blocked on your calendar after a meeting ends. This gives you time to wrap up, add notes, or decompress before your next commitment.

params.description: When enabled, users will be able to create a new booking when trying to reschedule a cancelled booking.

params.name: bookingLimitsDuration
params.description: Limit total amount of time that this event can be booked

params.name: bookingRequiresAuthentication
params.description: Boolean to require authentication for booking this event type via api. If true, only authenticated users who are the event-type owner or org/team admin/owner can book this event type.

params.name: bookingWindow
params.description: Limit how far in the future this event can be booked

params.name: confirmationPolicy
params.description: Specify how the booking needs to be manually confirmed before it is pushed to the integrations and a confirmation mail is sent.

params.name: customName

Customizable event name with valid variables:
      {Event type title}, {Organiser}, {Scheduler}, {Location}, {Organiser first name},
      {Scheduler first name}, {Scheduler last name}, {Event duration}, {LOCATION},
      {HOST/ATTENDEE}, {HOST}, {ATTENDEE}, {USER}

params.name: hideOrganizerEmail
params.description: Boolean to Hide organizer's email address from the booking screen, email notifications, and calendar events

params.name: interfaceLanguage
params.description: Set preferred language for the booking interface. Use empty string for visitor's browser language (default).

params.name: lengthInMinutesOptions
params.description: If you want that user can choose between different lengths of the event you can specify them here. Must include the provided `lengthInMinutes`.

params.name: lockTimeZoneToggleOnBookingPage
params.description: Lock time zone toggle on booking page

params.name: minimumBookingNotice
params.description: Minimum number of minutes before the event that a booking can be made.

params.name: offsetStart
params.description: Offset timeslots shown to bookers by a specified number of minutes

params.name: onlyShowFirstAvailableSlot
params.description: This will limit your availability for this event type to one slot per day, scheduled at the earliest available time.

params.name: rescheduleWithSameRoundRobinHost
params.description: Rescheduled events will be assigned to the same host as initially scheduled.

params.name: scheduleId
params.description: If you want that this event has different schedule than user's default one you can specify it here.

params.name: schedulingType
params.description:
The scheduling type for the team event - collective or roundRobin. ❗If you change scheduling type you must also provide `hosts` or `assignAllTeamMembers` in the request body, otherwise the event type will have no hosts - this is required because
      in case of collective event type all hosts are mandatory but in case of round robin some or non can be mandatory so we can't predict how you want the hosts to be setup which is why you must provide that information.  If you want to convert round robin or collective into managed or managed into round robin or collective then you will have to create a new team event type and delete old one.

params.name: seats
params.description: Create an event type with multiple seats.

params.name: showOptimizedSlots
params.description: Arrange time slots to optimize availability.

params.name: slotInterval
params.description:
Number representing length of each slot when event is booked. By default it equal length of the event type.
      If event length is 60 minutes then we would have slots 9AM, 10AM, 11AM etc. but if it was changed to 30 minutes then
      we would have slots 9AM, 9:30AM, 10AM, 10:30AM etc. as the available times to book the 60 minute event.

params.name: successRedirectUrl
params.description: A valid URL where the booker will redirect to, once the booking is completed successfully
